title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-02
summary: Today's dominant pattern is a sudden breakout in AI Agent infrastructure, specifically memory and UI layers. Hermes WebUI (+2,107 stars in 24h) and Supermemory (+939 stars) both show acceleration anomalies, indicating developer hunger for persistent memory and accessible agent interfaces. This is reinforced by sustained interest in workflow automation (Activepieces, n8n templates) and web scraping (Scrapling +1,888 stars), suggesting a broader push to make agents more autonomous and tool-capable. Meanwhile, Anthropic's confidential S-1 filing and Project Glasswing expansion appear on both Hacker News and cross-source signals, marking a rare convergence of corporate finance and open-source developer attention. The absence of major launches in Local LLM or RAG categories today suggests attention has shifted from model building to agent tooling and deployment.
dataNotes: GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。
daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。
Accelerating 只展示至少 3 天历史基线且今日 delta 超过前 3 日均值 2 倍的项目。
Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。

repository.name: hermes-webui
repository.description: Hermes WebUI: The best way to use Hermes Agent from the web or from your phone!

hotProjects.whyItMatters: AI Agent Framework 方向出现突然加速：24h stars +2107，前 3 日均值 546，加速度 1x，今天值得优先排查触发原因。
hotProjects.developerInsight: 可以用一个最小样例验证 API、文档和集成成本，再决定是否纳入长期技术观察。

llmSummary.oneLiner: A lightweight, dark-themed web UI for Hermes Agent that mirrors the CLI experience with persistent memory, scheduling, and multi-platform access.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Most AI tools reset every session, requiring users to re-explain context. Hermes WebUI provides a self-hosted, persistent agent with cross-session memory, scheduled jobs, and a full-featured web interface accessible from any device.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo gained 1,815 stars in the last 24 hours, reaching 12,002 total stars with 1,501 forks. Despite a low acceleration ratio (1.0), the sustained high velocity and recent release within 30 days indicate strong ongoing interest. The project is clearly resonating with developers seeking self-hosted, persistent AI agents.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and active commits suggest continuous improvement, and the README includes a detailed comparison table against competitors like OpenClaw and Claude Code, which may be driving awareness.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Hermes WebUI addresses a growing demand for self-hosted, always-on AI agents that remember context across sessions. Its feature parity with CLI, support for multiple providers, and integration with messaging platforms make it a compelling alternative to cloud-dependent tools.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: For developers building agentic workflows, Hermes WebUI offers a ready-to-deploy solution with persistent memory, scheduling, and a clean web interface. It's particularly useful for teams that need privacy, customization, and offline capabilities.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward self-hosted, memory-persistent AI agents as a practical alternative to stateless chat interfaces. Developers should consider how persistent context and scheduled automation can differentiate their own AI products.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and power users who want a self-hosted AI agent with persistent memory, scheduling, and multi-platform access, especially those already using Hermes Agent or seeking an open-source alternative to Claude Code or OpenClaw.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is tied to Hermes Agent, which may have its own adoption risks. Native Windows support is limited, and the community notes some POSIX path issues. The rapid star growth could attract maintenance challenges.

repository.description: AI Agents & MCPs & AI Workflow Automation • (~400 MCP servers for AI agents) • AI Automation / AI Agent with MCPs • AI Workflows & AI Agents • MCPs for AI Agents

hotProjects.whyItMatters: MCP / Tool Calling 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +84，总 stars 22,514，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。

llmSummary.oneLiner: Activepieces is an open-source, TypeScript-based AI workflow automation platform that doubles as a massive MCP server toolkit for AI agents, positioning itself as a Zapier/n8n alternative.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It replaces proprietary automation tools like Zapier with a self-hostable, extensible platform where all integrations (pieces) are open-source npm packages, and automatically become MCP servers usable by LLMs in Claude Desktop, Cursor, or Windsurf.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 22,514 stars, 3,735 forks, and a sustained 84-star delta in the last 24 hours, Activepieces is experiencing consistent, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating organic, steady interest rather than a sudden spike. This is a project that has already crossed the chasm into mainstream developer attention.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README emphasizes a recent shift to positioning all pieces as MCP servers, which aligns with the current AI agent tool-use trend.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines two hot trends: no-code workflow automation (Zapier replacement) and MCP servers for AI agents. The promise that contributing a piece automatically makes it an MCP server lowers the barrier for developers to build AI-compatible tools.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you build integrations, contributing to Activepieces gives you instant MCP server exposure. For teams, it offers a self-hosted, extensible automation backbone with AI-native features like human-in-the-loop and code pieces with NPM.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a convergence of automation platforms and AI tool ecosystems. Developers should watch for a future where every integration is also an MCP endpoint, reducing the need for separate tool-building efforts.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers building AI agents who need MCP servers, teams wanting self-hosted workflow automation, and contributors looking to publish open-source integrations that are automatically usable by LLMs.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 494 open issues, which may indicate growing pains. The enterprise features are under a commercial license, so full self-hosted parity requires careful license review. Competition from n8n and Zapier's AI features is intense.

repository.name: Scrapling
repository.description: 🕷️ An adaptive Web Scraping framework that handles everything from a single request to a full-scale crawl!

hotProjects.whyItMatters: MCP / Tool Calling 方向出现突然加速：24h stars +1888，前 3 日均值 756，加速度 1x，今天值得优先排查触发原因。
hotProjects.developerInsight: 重点看协议适配、工具注册和真实示例，判断它是否能降低 agent 接工具的成本。

llmSummary.oneLiner: An adaptive web scraping framework that auto-relocates elements when sites change, bypasses anti-bot systems, and scales from single requests to full crawls.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Web scrapers break when websites update their HTML structure, and anti-bot measures like Cloudflare Turnstile block traditional tools. Scrapling's parser learns from changes and automatically finds elements again, while its fetchers handle anti-bot bypass out of the box.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 57,984 stars and a 24-hour delta of 1,809, the project is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio is low (1), indicating steady organic interest rather than a sudden spike. This consistent attention suggests the project has become a go-to tool in the web scraping community.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits and a release within 30 days show active maintenance, but no external buzz or specific event is identifiable.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines adaptive parsing (auto-relocation of elements), anti-bot bypass, and a full spider framework in one library, addressing pain points that existing tools handle separately. The MCP server integration also taps into the AI agent ecosystem.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Scrapling reduces maintenance overhead for scrapers by automatically adapting to website changes, and its built-in anti-bot bypass simplifies data extraction from protected sites.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, Scrapling's MCP server and agent skill directory signal a shift toward making web scraping a first-class capability for AI agents, enabling autonomous data collection without constant manual selector updates.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Python developers building web scrapers, data extraction pipelines, or AI agents that need to collect data from modern websites with anti-bot protections.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new (first release likely in 2025) and may have undiscovered edge cases. Reliance on anti-bot bypass techniques could be fragile if target sites update their defenses. The README includes sponsored proxy services, which may introduce bias in recommendations.

repository.name: supermemory
repository.description: Memory engine and app that is extremely fast, scalable. The Memory API for the AI era.

hotProjects.whyItMatters: AI Agent Framework 方向出现突然加速：24h stars +939，前 3 日均值 388，加速度 1x，今天值得优先排查触发原因。
hotProjects.developerInsight: 可以用一个最小样例验证 API、文档和集成成本，再决定是否纳入长期技术观察。

llmSummary.oneLiner: Supermemory is an open-source memory and context engine for AI that tops major benchmarks, offering a single API for persistent memory, user profiles, hybrid search, and connectors.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI assistants forget everything between conversations, requiring complex setups of vector DBs, embedding pipelines, and chunking strategies. Supermemory solves this by providing a drop-in memory layer that automatically extracts facts, builds user profiles, handles contradictions, and delivers relevant context with one API call.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 24,420 stars and a 24-hour star delta of 939 (acceleration ratio 1, indicating sustained high velocity), Supermemory is experiencing a sudden breakout. The three-day average star delta of 388 and recent release within 30 days signal strong community adoption. It's #1 on three major AI memory benchmarks (LongMemEval, LoCoMo, ConvoMem), making it the current state-of-the-art in AI memory.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent release (within 30 days) and active commits (within 7 days) suggest ongoing improvements that maintain momentum.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Supermemory addresses a critical pain point for AI developers: persistent memory. Its benchmark leadership, simple API, and support for multiple frameworks (Vercel AI SDK, LangChain, Mastra, etc.) make it a compelling choice. The open-source nature and free consumer app also lower the barrier to entry.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Supermemory eliminates the need to build custom memory infrastructure. Developers can integrate persistent memory, user profiles, and RAG in minutes with a single SDK, reducing time-to-market for AI applications.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward commoditized memory layers for AI. Just as vector databases became a standard component, Supermemory is positioning itself as the default memory API. Developers should evaluate whether to adopt a managed memory service versus building in-house, as the cost of switching later may be high.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers building agents, chatbots, or personalization features; companies needing persistent user context; individual developers using AI tools like Claude, Cursor, or VS Code who want memory across sessions.
llmSummary.riskNotes: As a relatively young project (24k stars but still early), API stability and long-term maintenance are concerns. The project relies on Cloudflare Workers and Postgres, which may introduce vendor lock-in. Benchmark leadership is impressive but real-world performance may vary.

llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers need a reliable way to orchestrate long-running, fault-tolerant workflows across microservices without managing complex retry logic, state persistence, or distributed coordination manually.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 20,702 stars and a 24-hour delta of +162, Temporal shows strong, sustained community interest. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike. This consistent attention signals a mature, trusted platform that continues to attract new users daily.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits within 7 days and ongoing community engagement suggest continuous improvement without a specific breakout event.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Temporal addresses the growing need for reliable workflow orchestration in distributed systems, especially as AI and microservice architectures demand durable execution. Its mature ecosystem, strong documentation, and backing by Temporal Technologies drive steady adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Temporal is a production-grade choice for building resilient workflows. Its Go-based server and multi-language SDKs make it accessible for teams needing fault-tolerant orchestration without reinventing the wheel.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, Temporal provides a robust foundation for orchestrating AI pipelines, model training workflows, and data processing tasks that require durability and retry logic. It signals a shift toward reliable, stateful execution in AI applications.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Backend engineers, platform teams, and AI/ML engineers building distributed, fault-tolerant applications that require workflow orchestration with automatic retries and state management.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has a high number of open issues (753), which may indicate maintenance challenges or feature backlog. However, the active community and regular commits mitigate this risk.

modelDemoSignals.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
modelDemoSignals.description: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response
modelDemoSignals.summary: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response text instead of their own visual perception, leading to inconsistent and non-verifiable evaluations. To address this issue, we introduce the Perceptually Perturbed Judgment Dataset, which constructs minimally edited counterfactual responses that isolate perceptual errors and enable verifiable supervision. Building on this dataset, we develop a unified training framework that combines a structured GRPO-based reward with a batch-ranking objective, achieving coherent global ordering without explicit pairwise labels. Experiments across diverse MLLM-as-a-Judge benchmarks show that our approach substantially improves perceptual fidelity, ranking coherence, and alignment with human evaluation. Our results establish a scalable and generalizable pathway for training multimodal judges that are perceptually grounded, interpretable, and robust to visual-reasoning conflicts.
modelDemoSignals.author: Seojeong Park, Jiho Choi, Junyong Kang, Seonho Lee, Jaeyo Shin, Hyunjung Shim
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, alignment, benchmark, evaluation

modelDemoSignals.title: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
modelDemoSignals.description: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong performance through instruction tuning, but real-world deployment requires them to continually acquire new vision-language capabilities, making Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) essential. modelDemoSignals.summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong performance through instruction tuning, but real-world deployment requires them to continually acquire new vision-language capabilities, making Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) essential. To reduce inter-task interference and promote collaboration, recent methods often employ sparse architectures like Mixture of LoRA Experts with image-text similarity routing. However, tasks with distinct response structures could share highly similar visual-linguistic semantics and thus be wrongly routed to the same expert; image-text similarity alone is insufficient for reliable task assignment. For example, an expert in a grounding task requiring coordinate prediction may be biased toward producing short textual answers after learning semantically similar VQA tasks. This format-blind task assignment integrates heterogeneous response types into shared parameters, inducing gradient interference and ineffective expert collaboration. To address this problem, we propose ProtoAda, a prototype-guided adaptive tuning framework. ProtoAda introduces format-aware task prototypes to align task assignment and routing with both task semantics and output structure, and further consolidates format-compatible updates in a geometry-aware manner to effectively reuse and progressively refine existing parameters. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that ProtoAda achieves superior performance, especially on tasks whose answer structures are easily corrupted by sequential tuning.
modelDemoSignals.author: Yu-Cheng Shi, Zhen-Hao Xie, Jun-Tao Tang, Da-Wei Zhou
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, benchmark

modelDemoSignals.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
modelDemoSignals.description: Video is temporally redundant: adjacent frames usually share most objects, background, and layout. Yet existing video multimodal large language models (video MLLMs) usually encode each sampled frame as an independent RGB image, causing visual tokens to repeat content already present in earlier frames. This suggests a more direct video interface: send a full reference frame only when the scene cannot be predicted well from prior context, and otherwise transmit a compact description of inter-frame
modelDemoSignals.summary: Video is temporally redundant: adjacent frames usually share most objects, background, and layout. Yet existing video multimodal large language models (video MLLMs) usually encode each sampled frame as an independent RGB image, causing visual tokens to repeat content already present in earlier frames. This suggests a more direct video interface: send a full reference frame only when the scene cannot be predicted well from prior context, and otherwise transmit a compact description of inter-frame changes. We call this interface a \emph{predictive visual code}, and instantiate it for video MLLMs as \textbf{AdaCodec}. AdaCodec spends full visual tokens on a reference frame only when its conditional predictive cost is high; otherwise, it encodes inter-frame changes, including motion and prediction residuals, as compact P-tokens. Across all eleven benchmarks, AdaCodec improves over the Qwen3-VL-8B per-frame RGB baseline at a matched visual-token budget. Even at $1/7$ the budget, AdaCodec with 32k tokens surpasses the 224k baseline on all long-video benchmarks; on five general-video benchmarks, it raises the average score while substantially cutting time-to-first-token from 9.26s to 1.62s.
modelDemoSignals.author: Haowen Hou, Zhen Huang, Zheming Liang, Qingyi Si, Chenglin Li, Shuai Dong, Kele Shao, Ruilin Li, Dianyi Wang, Nan Duan, Jiaqi Wang
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, rag, benchmark

modelDemoSignals.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
modelDemoSignals.description: Clinical practice is not the selection of an answer from enumerated options: a physician gathers heterogeneous information incrementally and commits to sequential, irreversible decisions under uncertainty. Static benchmarks cannot probe and existing interactive medical benchmarks each compromise on at least one of them. We present ClinEnv, an interactive benchmark that evaluates LLMs as attending physicians over real inpatient admissions under a paradigm we term Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation
modelDemoSignals.summary: Clinical practice is not the selection of an answer from enumerated options: a physician gathers heterogeneous information incrementally and commits to sequential, irreversible decisions under uncertainty. Static benchmarks cannot probe and existing interactive medical benchmarks each compromise on at least one of them. We present ClinEnv, an interactive benchmark that evaluates LLMs as attending physicians over real inpatient admissions under a paradigm we term Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation. Each case is automatically constructed into an ordered sequence of decision stages; at every stage the model must actively query four specialized agents before committing to medications, procedures, and diagnoses. ClinEnv scores both what the model decides, through deterministic ontology-grounded matching, and how it gathers information. Across seven models, the strongest reaches only 0.31 decision F1, and outcome quality is sharply decoupled from process quality. Difficulty concentrates in management decisions and later stages, where models recover discharge diagnoses far more reliably than management actions (0.51 vs. 0.17 F1) and continue to issue redundant queries as cases progress. ClinEnv makes this information-acquisition gap, invisible to outcome-only evaluation, directly measurable.
modelDemoSignals.author: Yuxing Lu, Yushuhong Lin, Wenqi Shi, J. Ben Tamo, Xukai Zhao, Jinzhuo Wang, May Dongmei Wang
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, benchmark, evaluation

modelDemoSignals.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
modelDemoSignals.description: Autonomous robots that interact with people must make safe and efficient decisions under human-induced uncertainty, such as their preferences, goals, competency, and willingness to cooperate. Safety filters are a popular approach for ensuring safety in interactive robotics, since their modular design separates safety from performance, allowing robots to operate safely around people with minimal impact on task efficiency. While traditional safety filters typically operate only in the physical spa
modelDemoSignals.summary: Autonomous robots that interact with people must make safe and efficient decisions under human-induced uncertainty, such as their preferences, goals, competency, and willingness to cooperate. Safety filters are a popular approach for ensuring safety in interactive robotics, since their modular design separates safety from performance, allowing robots to operate safely around people with minimal impact on task efficiency. While traditional safety filters typically operate only in the physical space, neglecting the robot's ability to learn and adapt online, the recently proposed belief-space safety filter (BeliefSF) reasons about robot safety in closed-loop with runtime inference that actively reduces the robot's uncertainty online, thereby reducing conservativeness in filtering. However, providing formal safety guarantees for robots deploying BeliefSF remains a significant challenge due to errors in runtime inference and neural approximation of safety filters required to handle the high dimensionality of belief spaces. In this paper, we propose an algorithmic approach to certify high-probability safety of BeliefSF using conformal prediction, while explicitly accounting for the reliability of the robot's runtime inference module. Our method leverages the structure of belief-space safety filtering by focusing verification on a region where inference is expected to be reliable. It preserves the simplicity and sample complexity of standard conformal prediction, yet can certify a substantially less conservative safety filter. Through a simulated human-vehicle interaction benchmark, we show that our approach verifies a significantly more permissive belief-space safety filter than a standard conformal prediction baseline.
modelDemoSignals.author: Haimin Hu
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, safety, benchmark

aihotHighlights.title: Anthropic扩展Project Glasswing计划
aihotHighlights.summary: Anthropic正将其Project Glasswing计划扩展至约150个新组织，此前首批约50个合作伙伴。新伙伴分布于十五个多国家，覆盖电力、水务、医疗、通信和硬件等关键基础设施行业。这些合作伙伴的共同点在于，其代码库若遭成功攻击，后果可能极其严重，影响或超1亿人。项目旨在利用Claude Mythos Preview等前沿模型扫描漏洞并协助修复，以应对AI驱动的网络安全挑战。同时，Anthropic推出了基于Claude Opus 4.8等公开模型的Claude Security产品，用于扫描代码并建议补丁。

aihotHighlights.title: Anthropic可解释性研究：区分因果效应相似的特征
aihotHighlights.summary: Anthropic可解释性团队介绍了其Circuits研究的新进展。为区分那些激活模式相似但因果效应不同的模型特征，团队提出一种新方法。该方法通过分析特征的下游连接来预测其实际影响，并使用基于共激活统计的TWERA（虚拟权重）对连接进行加权排序。实验表明，借助下游连接信息能更准确地判断哪个特征会引导特定输出。此方法为识别模型内部真正的因果组件提供了新途径。

aihotHighlights.title: SK会长崔泰源：SK海力士计划未来五年内晶圆产能翻倍
aihotHighlights.summary: SK海力士会长崔泰源宣布，计划在未来五年内将整体晶圆产能提高一倍，以应对AI普及带来的持续存储供应短缺。他预测AI数据中心和AI PC的普及将持续拉动存储需求，供需紧张局面可能延续至2030年。SK海力士将投入大规模资金用于设备、建设等扩张，尽管面临前置时间长（新建晶圆厂至少三年）和资源成本上涨等挑战。目前，SK海力士市值已首次突破1万亿美元。

aihotHighlights.title: 为了不花那120刀，我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill
aihotHighlights.summary: 作者受一条推文启发，使用Codex对自己的MacBook进行了只读存储分析，发现了B站缓存视频等大量可清理空间（激进方案超140G）。为替代收费软件CleanMyMac，作者制作并开源了一个跨平台（支持Mac/Windows）的AI清理skill。该skill会扫描文件并生成可交互的HTML报告，通过三色分级（绿灯可放心清理、黄灯需人工判断、红灯禁止动）直观展示，并提供安全执行按钮。实测清理后释放了近120G空间，相比CleanMyMac仅扫描出的15.8G，其信息更透明、建议更详细。

aihotHighlights.title: 阶跃星辰Step 3.7 Flash发布，专为高效推理设计
aihotHighlights.summary: 阶跃星辰发布其推理优化型模型Step 3.7 Flash。该模型为196B MoE架构，从设计之初就专注于推理效率。其采用多矩阵分解注意力机制，使KV-cache成本仅为DeepSeek模型的约22%；同时通过注意力与FFN解耦技术，实现了硬件优化的高效服务。该模型已通过Fireworks AI提供，采用Apache 2.0许可，并可用于构建智能体应用。

aihotHighlights.title: Anthropic 开发者分享 Claude Code「理解验证」工作流
aihotHighlights.summary: Anthropic 核心开发者分享了一套用于 Claude Code 的「理解验证」工作流。该工作流将 AI 定位为"高效且睿智的教师"，成功标准不仅是完成任务，更是确保人类对问题、方案及影响有可复述、可辩护的掌握。它通过增量教学、用户复述、清单+测验等方式，围绕问题域、方案域和语境域三条轴线展开，具体包含8个可执行步骤，强调在进入下一阶段前需确认用户已真正理解。此工作流旨在对抗长会话中人类易沦为"审批按钮"的"智能体黑箱"问题，强制沉淀决策上下文，实现可审计的深度理解。

aihotHighlights.title: Sam Altman强调AI发展应以人为本
aihotHighlights.summary: Sam Altman在采访中表示，AI不应被设计为追求脱离人类需求的目标，人类必须始终处于AI发展的中心。他批判了行业内"AI将摧毁大量工作"等言论，认为人们担忧的并非AI带来的好处，而是自身在未来的角色、经济前景与自主权。他指出，AI行业的失败在于未能清晰解释人类如何在每一步保持对未来的控制权，以及如何在AI时代继续拥有充实、有意义的生活。

crossSourceHighlights.title: Anthropic 保密向 SEC 提交 S-1 草案
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic 保密向 sec 提交 s 1 草案
title:anthropic confidentially submits draft s 1 to the sec

items.title: Anthropic 保密向 SEC 提交 S-1 草案
items.summary: Anthropic， PBC 今日保密向美国证券交易委员会提交了 S-1 表格草案，计划进行普通股的首次公开发行。这使其在 SEC 完成审核后拥有上市的选择权。IPO 的具体发行股数和价格尚未确定，将取决于市场条件等因素。公司近期刚完成由 Altimeter Capital 等领投的 650 亿美元 H 轮融资，估值达 9650 亿美元，并发布了 Claude Opus 4.8 模型。

crossSourceHighlights.title: Anthropic扩展Project Glasswing计划

items.title: Anthropic扩展Project Glasswing计划
items.summary: Anthropic正将其Project Glasswing计划扩展至约150个新组织，此前首批约50个合作伙伴。新伙伴分布于十五个多国家，覆盖电力、水务、医疗、通信和硬件等关键基础设施行业。这些合作伙伴的共同点在于，其代码库若遭成功攻击，后果可能极其严重，影响或超1亿人。项目旨在利用Claude Mythos Preview等前沿模型扫描漏洞并协助修复，以应对AI驱动的网络安全挑战。同时，Anthropic推出了基于Claude Opus 4.8等公开模型的Claude Security产品，用于扫描代码并建议补丁。

multiSourceItems.title: Nathan Lambert离开Ai2，结束2.5年OLMO等项目工作
multiSourceItems.summary: Ai2（Allen Institute for AI）研究员Nathan Lambert宣布离职。他在Ai2工作超过2.5年，期间主导或参与了OLMO和Tulu等开源模型项目，称其为职业生涯的巅峰。他表示将暂时休息，未来仍会继续深耕开源模型与开放科学领域。

multiSourceItems.title: Anthropic可解释性研究：区分因果效应相似的特征
multiSourceItems.summary: Anthropic可解释性团队介绍了其Circuits研究的新进展。为区分那些激活模式相似但因果效应不同的模型特征，团队提出一种新方法。该方法通过分析特征的下游连接来预测其实际影响，并使用基于共激活统计的TWERA（虚拟权重）对连接进行加权排序。实验表明，借助下游连接信息能更准确地判断哪个特征会引导特定输出。此方法为识别模型内部真正的因果组件提供了新途径。

multiSourceItems.title: SK会长崔泰源：SK海力士计划未来五年内晶圆产能翻倍
multiSourceItems.summary: SK海力士会长崔泰源宣布，计划在未来五年内将整体晶圆产能提高一倍，以应对AI普及带来的持续存储供应短缺。他预测AI数据中心和AI PC的普及将持续拉动存储需求，供需紧张局面可能延续至2030年。SK海力士将投入大规模资金用于设备、建设等扩张，尽管面临前置时间长（新建晶圆厂至少三年）和资源成本上涨等挑战。目前，SK海力士市值已首次突破1万亿美元。

multiSourceItems.title: 为了不花那120刀，我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill
multiSourceItems.summary: 作者受一条推文启发，使用Codex对自己的MacBook进行了只读存储分析，发现了B站缓存视频等大量可清理空间（激进方案超140G）。为替代收费软件CleanMyMac，作者制作并开源了一个跨平台（支持Mac/Windows）的AI清理skill。该skill会扫描文件并生成可交互的HTML报告，通过三色分级（绿灯可放心清理、黄灯需人工判断、红灯禁止动）直观展示，并提供安全执行按钮。实测清理后释放了近120G空间，相比CleanMyMac仅扫描出的15.8G，其信息更透明、建议更详细。

multiSourceItems.title: 阶跃星辰Step 3.7 Flash发布，专为高效推理设计
multiSourceItems.summary: 阶跃星辰发布其推理优化型模型Step 3.7 Flash。该模型为196B MoE架构，从设计之初就专注于推理效率。其采用多矩阵分解注意力机制，使KV-cache成本仅为DeepSeek模型的约22%；同时通过注意力与FFN解耦技术，实现了硬件优化的高效服务。该模型已通过Fireworks AI提供，采用Apache 2.0许可，并可用于构建智能体应用。

multiSourceItems.title: Anthropic 开发者分享 Claude Code「理解验证」工作流
multiSourceItems.summary: Anthropic 核心开发者分享了一套用于 Claude Code 的「理解验证」工作流。该工作流将 AI 定位为"高效且睿智的教师"，成功标准不仅是完成任务，更是确保人类对问题、方案及影响有可复述、可辩护的掌握。它通过增量教学、用户复述、清单+测验等方式，围绕问题域、方案域和语境域三条轴线展开，具体包含8个可执行步骤，强调在进入下一阶段前需确认用户已真正理解。此工作流旨在对抗长会话中人类易沦为"审批按钮"的"智能体黑箱"问题，强制沉淀决策上下文，实现可审计的深度理解。

multiSourceItems.title: Sam Altman强调AI发展应以人为本
multiSourceItems.summary: Sam Altman在采访中表示，AI不应被设计为追求脱离人类需求的目标，人类必须始终处于AI发展的中心。他批判了行业内"AI将摧毁大量工作"等言论，认为人们担忧的并非AI带来的好处，而是自身在未来的角色、经济前景与自主权。他指出，AI行业的失败在于未能清晰解释人类如何在每一步保持对未来的控制权，以及如何在AI时代继续拥有充实、有意义的生活。

multiSourceItems.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
multiSourceItems.description: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response
multiSourceItems.summary: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response text instead of their own visual perception, leading to inconsistent and non-verifiable evaluations. To address this issue, we introduce the Perceptually Perturbed Judgment Dataset, which constructs minimally edited counterfactual responses that isolate perceptual errors and enable verifiable supervision. Building on this dataset, we develop a unified training framework that combines a structured GRPO-based reward with a batch-ranking objective, achieving coherent global ordering without explicit pairwise labels. Experiments across diverse MLLM-as-a-Judge benchmarks show that our approach substantially improves perceptual fidelity, ranking coherence, and alignment with human evaluation. Our results establish a scalable and generalizable pathway for training multimodal judges that are perceptually grounded, interpretable, and robust to visual-reasoning conflicts.
multiSourceItems.author: Seojeong Park, Jiho Choi, Junyong Kang, Seonho Lee, Jaeyo Shin, Hyunjung Shim

multiSourceItems.title: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
multiSourceItems.description: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong performance through instruction tuning, but real-world deployment requires them to continually acquire new vision-language capabilities, making Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) essential. To reduce inter-task interference and promote collaboration, recent methods often employ sparse architectures like Mixture of LoRA Experts with image-text similarity routing. multiSourceItems.summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong performance through instruction tuning, but real-world deployment requires them to continually acquire new vision-language capabilities, making Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) essential. To reduce inter-task interference and promote collaboration, recent methods often employ sparse architectures like Mixture of LoRA Experts with image-text similarity routing. However, tasks with distinct response structures could share highly similar visual-linguistic semantics and thus be wrongly routed to the same expert; image-text similarity alone is insufficient for reliable task assignment. For example, an expert in a grounding task requiring coordinate prediction may be biased toward producing short textual answers after learning semantically similar VQA tasks. This format-blind task assignment integrates heterogeneous response types into shared parameters, inducing gradient interference and ineffective expert collaboration. To address this problem, we propose ProtoAda, a prototype-guided adaptive tuning framework. ProtoAda introduces format-aware task prototypes to align task assignment and routing with both task semantics and output structure, and further consolidates format-compatible updates in a geometry-aware manner to effectively reuse and progressively refine existing parameters. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that ProtoAda achieves superior performance, especially on tasks whose answer structures are easily corrupted by sequential tuning.
multiSourceItems.author: Yu-Cheng Shi, Zhen-Hao Xie, Jun-Tao Tang, Da-Wei Zhou

multiSourceItems.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
multiSourceItems.description: Video is temporally redundant: adjacent frames usually share most objects, background, and layout. Yet existing video multimodal large language models (video MLLMs) usually encode each sampled frame as an independent RGB image, causing visual tokens to repeat content already present in earlier frames. This suggests a more direct video interface: send a full reference frame only when the scene cannot be predicted well from prior context, and otherwise transmit a compact description of inter-frame
multiSourceItems.summary: Video is temporally redundant: adjacent frames usually share most objects, background, and layout. Yet existing video multimodal large language models (video MLLMs) usually encode each sampled frame as an independent RGB image, causing visual tokens to repeat content already present in earlier frames. This suggests a more direct video interface: send a full reference frame only when the scene cannot be predicted well from prior context, and otherwise transmit a compact description of inter-frame changes. We call this interface a \emph{predictive visual code}, and instantiate it for video MLLMs as \textbf{AdaCodec}. AdaCodec spends full visual tokens on a reference frame only when its conditional predictive cost is high; otherwise, it encodes inter-frame changes, including motion and prediction residuals, as compact P-tokens. Across all eleven benchmarks, AdaCodec improves over the Qwen3-VL-8B per-frame RGB baseline at a matched visual-token budget. Even at $1/7$ the budget, AdaCodec with 32k tokens surpasses the 224k baseline on all long-video benchmarks; on five general-video benchmarks, it raises the average score while substantially cutting time-to-first-token from 9.26s to 1.62s.
multiSourceItems.author: Haowen Hou, Zhen Huang, Zheming Liang, Qingyi Si, Chenglin Li, Shuai Dong, Kele Shao, Ruilin Li, Dianyi Wang, Nan Duan, Jiaqi Wang

multiSourceItems.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
multiSourceItems.description: Clinical practice is not the selection of an answer from enumerated options: a physician gathers heterogeneous information incrementally and commits to sequential, irreversible decisions under uncertainty. Static benchmarks cannot probe and existing interactive medical benchmarks each compromise on at least one of them. We present ClinEnv, an interactive benchmark that evaluates LLMs as attending physicians over real inpatient admissions under a paradigm we term Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation
multiSourceItems.summary: Clinical practice is not the selection of an answer from enumerated options: a physician gathers heterogeneous information incrementally and commits to sequential, irreversible decisions under uncertainty. Static benchmarks cannot probe and existing interactive medical benchmarks each compromise on at least one of them. We present ClinEnv, an interactive benchmark that evaluates LLMs as attending physicians over real inpatient admissions under a paradigm we term Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation. Each case is automatically constructed into an ordered sequence of decision stages; at every stage the model must actively query four specialized agents before committing to medications, procedures, and diagnoses. ClinEnv scores both what the model decides, through deterministic ontology-grounded matching, and how it gathers information. Across seven models, the strongest reaches only 0.31 decision F1, and outcome quality is sharply decoupled from process quality. Difficulty concentrates in management decisions and later stages, where models recover discharge diagnoses far more reliably than management actions (0.51 vs. 0.17 F1) and continue to issue redundant queries as cases progress. ClinEnv makes this information-acquisition gap, invisible to outcome-only evaluation, directly measurable.

multiSourceItems.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
multiSourceItems.description: Autonomous robots that interact with people must make safe and efficient decisions under human-induced uncertainty, such as their preferences, goals, competency, and willingness to cooperate. Safety filters are a popular approach for ensuring safety in interactive robotics, since their modular design separates safety from performance, allowing robots to operate safely around people with minimal impact on task efficiency. While traditional safety filters typically operate only in the physical spa
multiSourceItems.summary: Autonomous robots that interact with people must make safe and efficient decisions under human-induced uncertainty, such as their preferences, goals, competency, and willingness to cooperate. Safety filters are a popular approach for ensuring safety in interactive robotics, since their modular design separates safety from performance, allowing robots to operate safely around people with minimal impact on task efficiency. While traditional safety filters typically operate only in the physical space, neglecting the robot's ability to learn and adapt online, the recently proposed belief-space safety filter (BeliefSF) reasons about robot safety in closed-loop with runtime inference that actively reduces the robot's uncertainty online, thereby reducing conservativeness in filtering. However, providing formal safety guarantees for robots deploying BeliefSF remains a significant challenge due to errors in runtime inference and neural approximation of safety filters required to handle the high dimensionality of belief spaces. In this paper, we propose an algorithmic approach to certify high-probability safety of BeliefSF using conformal prediction, while explicitly accounting for the reliability of the robot's runtime inference module. Our method leverages the structure of belief-space safety filtering by focusing verification on a region where inference is expected to be reliable. It preserves the simplicity and sample complexity of standard conformal prediction, yet can certify a substantially less conservative safety filter. Through a simulated human-vehicle interaction benchmark, we show that our approach verifies a significantly more permissive belief-space safety filter than a standard conformal prediction baseline.
multiSourceItems.author: Haimin Hu
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, safety, benchmark

multiSourceItems.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
multiSourceItems.description: Post-training compression of Large Language Models (LLMs) removes entire architectural components, either deleting them or replacing them with fitted modules. Existing replacement-based methods share two design constraints: full-layer granularity and contiguous selection. We argue that this is overly restrictive: in fact, redundancy in pretrained transformers is not confined to contiguous regions, nor does it evenly distribute between Attention and FeedForward outputs, implying that different st
multiSourceItems.summary: Post-training compression of Large Language Models (LLMs) removes entire architectural components, either deleting them or replacing them with fitted modules. Existing replacement-based methods share two design constraints: full-layer granularity and contiguous selection. We argue that this is overly restrictive: in fact, redundancy in pretrained transformers is not confined to contiguous regions, nor does it evenly distribute between Attention and FeedForward outputs, implying that different strategies best approximate different submodule types and that removable components need not cluster within contiguous depth ranges. Based on this intuition, we introduce SubFit (Submodule-level Fitted residual replacement), which compresses LLMs at the submodule level: Attention and FeedForward submodules are selected non-contiguously, and each receives its own lightweight fitted residual bypass. SubFit operates post-training and requires only calibration data. Across ten LLMs (five base, five instruction-tuned), five sparsity levels from 12.5% to 37.5%, and four replacement-based baselines, SubFit achieves the best aggregate perplexity-accuracy trade-off across the evaluated sparsity levels, with larger gains under aggressive compression. At 25% sparsity, it retains 84.6% of dense downstream accuracy and incurs 2.42x perplexity degradation, against 81.6% and 4.34x for the strongest baselines, while delivering measurable inference speedup and KV-cache savings. Code is available at https://github.com/eliacunegatti/SubFit.
multiSourceItems.author: Elia Cunegatti, Marcus Vukojevic, Erik Nielsen, Giovanni Iacca
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

multiSourceItems.title: HERO'S JOURNEY: Testing Complex Rule Induction with Text Games
multiSourceItems.description: We introduce HERO'S JOURNEY, a benchmark for rule induction in goal-directed episodic tasks, where agents must infer hidden rules from demonstrations and act on them through multi-step execution. HERO'S JOURNEY covers eight tasks across attribute and procedural induction families, each with four structural rule forms, controllable lexical grounding, and identifiability conditions. Evaluating state-of-the-art LLMs, we find that models show evidence of rule induction, but the ability is limited an
multiSourceItems.summary: We introduce HERO'S JOURNEY, a benchmark for rule induction in goal-directed episodic tasks, where agents must infer hidden rules from demonstrations and act on them through multi-step execution. HERO'S JOURNEY covers eight tasks across attribute and procedural induction families, each with four structural rule forms, controllable lexical grounding, and identifiability conditions. Evaluating state-of-the-art LLMs, we find that models show evidence of rule induction, but the ability is limited and uneven across tasks. Meanwhile, process execution adds an execution bottleneck for models, whereas surface semantics has minimal effect. Induction-specific steering methods improve performance on attribute tasks but show no reliable gains on procedural tasks, suggesting the gap in procedural induction remains an open challenge.
multiSourceItems.author: Anshun Asher Zheng, Kanishka Misra, David I. Beaver, Junyi Jessy Li

multiSourceItems.title: SN-WER: Script-Normalized WER for Multi-Script Indic ASR Evaluation
multiSourceItems.description: Word Error Rate (WER) is the dominant metric for automatic speech recognition (ASR), but it can overestimate errors when references and hypotheses encode the same words in different scripts. This issue is common in multilingual settings where ASR models may emit romanized text. We propose Script-Normalized WER (SN-WER), a training-free, evaluation-only scoring method that transliterates both reference and hypothesis text into a language-specific canonical script before computing WER. We evaluate
multiSourceItems.summary: Word Error Rate (WER) is the dominant metric for automatic speech recognition (ASR), but it can overestimate errors when references and hypotheses encode the same words in different scripts. This issue is common in multilingual settings where ASR models may emit romanized text. We propose Script-Normalized WER (SN-WER), a training-free, evaluation-only scoring method that transliterates both reference and hypothesis text into a language-specific canonical script before computing WER. We evaluate SN-WER on 5 Indic languages, 2 datasets, and 3 ASR models. On curated FLEURS data, SN-WER reduces inflated model gaps by up to 12%, while on noisier Common Voice data the reductions are smaller or inconsistent, indicating genuine recognition weaknesses rather than only script mismatch. Controlled stress tests show a 67% attenuation of artificial romanization-induced WER inflation, while lexical-substitution controls show near-identical sensitivity to semantic errors, with Delta SN-WER / Delta WER approximately 1.09. SN-WER is robust to transliterator choice, normalization changes, and shows low token-collision rates below 0.1% in the evaluated Indic setting. We argue that SN-WER should be reported alongside WER and CER as a companion metric for script-insensitive ASR evaluation, especially when transcripts feed downstream search, indexing, or multilingual LLM pipelines.
multiSourceItems.author: Priyaranjan Pattnayak
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, evaluation

multiSourceItems.title: Transferable Self-Harm Surveillance from Emergency Department Triage Notes Using an Evidence-Augmented Machine Learning Approach
multiSourceItems.description: Self-harm is a major public health concern, but current surveillance relying on hospital presentations is inadequate due to the low sensitivity of diagnostic codes. Emergency Department (ED) triage notes, recorded at the initial point of contact, provide a succinct summary of presentations and an opportunity to identify self-harm. We developed a three-stage approach, augmenting traditional machine learning with large language model-based screening and evidence extraction to detect self-harm in E
multiSourceItems.summary: Self-harm is a major public health concern, but current surveillance relying on hospital presentations is inadequate due to the low sensitivity of diagnostic codes. Emergency Department (ED) triage notes, recorded at the initial point of contact, provide a succinct summary of presentations and an opportunity to identify self-harm. We developed a three-stage approach, augmenting traditional machine learning with large language model-based screening and evidence extraction to detect self-harm in ED triage notes. We assessed model transferability across three Australian hospitals. Our approach showed AUPRCs of 0.887 +/- 0.016 and 0.884 +/- 0.012 during internal and external validation. Prospectively, it achieved AUPRC of 0.881 +/- 0.008 at the development site, and 0.879 +/- 0.012 and 0.816 +/- 0.015 at two external sites without site-specific retraining. A key advantage of the approach is that it enables identification of the primary self-harm method with an accuracy of 95%, supporting more granular surveillance beyond binary classification.
multiSourceItems.author: Liuliu Chen, Gowri Rajaram, Eleanor Bailey, Katrina Witt, Michelle Lamblin, Jo Robinson, Mike Conway, Vlada Rozova
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model

multiSourceItems.title: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
multiSourceItems.description: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) have recently emerged as a promising alternative to autoregressive (AR) LLMs, offering faster inference through parallel or blockwise decoding. However, their masked language modeling formulation remains incompatible with standard token-level speculative decoding, one of the most effective acceleration techniques for AR models. In AR decoding, the causal mask preserves temporally valid token-level contexts, enabling a target model to verify multiple drafte
multiSourceItems.summary: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) have recently emerged as a promising alternative to autoregressive (AR) LLMs, offering faster inference through parallel or blockwise decoding. However, their masked language modeling formulation remains incompatible with standard token-level speculative decoding, one of the most effective acceleration techniques for AR models. In AR decoding, the causal mask preserves temporally valid token-level contexts, enabling a target model to verify multiple drafted tokens in a single forward pass. In contrast, dLLMs rely on mask tokens and bidirectional attention, causing the effective context to change across denoising steps and preventing direct token-level speculative verification. To bridge this gap, we propose a simple but effective speculative decoding algorithm for diffusion language models, named SimSD, which mainly adopts a plug-and-play masking strategy that equips dLLMs with temporally valid token-level contexts for speculative decoding. Our method explicitly introduces reference tokens from draft-model predictions and designs an attention mask that regulates their interaction with current-step tokens, allowing dLLMs to compute valid logits for drafted tokens in a single forward pass. This restores the key verification ability provided by causal masking in AR models while preserving the parallel decoding advantages of dLLMs. The proposed method is training-free and can be flexibly integrated with other acceleration techniques such as KV cache and blockwise decoding. Experiments on SDAR-family dLLMs across four benchmarks show that our method achieves up to 7.46x higher decoding throughput while maintaining and even improving average generation quality.
multiSourceItems.author: Junxia Cui, Haotian Ye, Runchu Tian, Hongcan Guo, Jinya Jiang, Haoru Li, Chaojie Ren, Yiming Huang, Kaijie Zhu, Zhongkai Yu, Kun Zhou, Jingbo Shang
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, rag, benchmark

multiSourceItems.title: SkillHarm: Lifecycle-Aware Skill-Based Attacks via Automated Construction
multiSourceItems.description: Agent skills occupy a privileged position in the agent workflow, as agents are expected to implicitly follow and execute them, rendering third-party skills a vulnerable attack surface. Existing studies have revealed unsafe agent behaviors induced by skill-based attacks, but they primarily evaluate poisoned skills within a single task execution and enumerate harms through ad-hoc risk lists. To bridge these gaps, we introduce SkillHarm, a benchmark of skill-based attacks across the skill-use lifec
multiSourceItems.summary: Agent skills occupy a privileged position in the agent workflow, as agents are expected to implicitly follow and execute them, rendering third-party skills a vulnerable attack surface. Existing studies have revealed unsafe agent behaviors induced by skill-based attacks, but they primarily evaluate poisoned skills within a single task execution and enumerate harms through ad-hoc risk lists. To bridge these gaps, we introduce SkillHarm, a benchmark of skill-based attacks across the skill-use lifecycle, paired with a systematic taxonomy of skill-relevant risks. SkillHarm evaluates two attack scenarios: Fixed-Payload Poisoning (FPP), where a fixed poisoned skill package directly compromises any task session that invokes it, and Self-Mutating Poisoning (SMP), where an initially benign execution silently mutates persistent skill content, deferring harm until a subsequent reuse. It further defines 12 risk types based on the agent workflow component targeted by the harm: data pipelines, system environments, and agent autonomy. To instantiate these attacks at scale, we build AutoSkillHarm, an automated construction pipeline with coding agents driven by natural-language harnesses. The resulting benchmark contains 879 attack samples across 71 skills. Experiments show that current agents remain vulnerable with attack success rates up to 86.3% in FPP and 69.3% in SMP. Our analysis further reveals a latent risk: many apparent attack failures stem from the agent failing to engage with the poisoned file rather than genuine resistance, and current defenses still fail to reliably mitigate the threat.
multiSourceItems.author: Yuting Ning, Zhehao Zhang, Yash Kumar Lal, Boyu Gou, Junyi Li, Weitong Ruan, Chentao Ye, Rahul Gupta, Diyi Yang, Yu Su, Huan Sun
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, coding agent, benchmark, workflow

multiSourceItems.title: Tracking the Behavioral Trajectories of Adapting Agents
multiSourceItems.description: Text files such as skill files, memory files, and behavioral configuration files play a central role in defining how modern agents act. Through edits by humans or the agents themselves, these files may evolve over time, directly steering the agent's behavior in future interactions. We present a methodology and framework for measuring agent $traits$ by defining traits as directions in the embedding space of a text embedding model. We train a linear model on labeled "before" versus "after" skill f
multiSourceItems.summary: Text files such as skill files, memory files, and behavioral configuration files play a central role in defining how modern agents act. Through edits by humans or the agents themselves, these files may evolve over time, directly steering the agent's behavior in future interactions. We present a methodology and framework for measuring agent $traits$ by defining traits as directions in the embedding space of a text embedding model. We train a linear model on labeled "before" versus "after" skill file diffs to learn a trait vector, then score arbitrary skill edits by projecting their embedding diffs onto this vector. Evaluated on 68 labeled skill diff pairs for the trait of propensity to seek sensitive data, our method achieves 91.2% sign classification accuracy and a Spearman rank correlation of $ρ= 0.82$ under leave-one-out cross-validation. We build this trait evaluation into a broader agent-to-agent protocol that enables one agent to evaluate another's skill file updates through a trusted intermediary.
multiSourceItems.author: Jonah Leshin, Manish Shah, Ian Timmis
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, embedding, evaluation

multiSourceItems.title: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment
multiSourceItems.description: Aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) with human values often degrades their general capabilities, termed the alignment tax. Existing methods mitigate this by balancing dual objectives, which heavily rely on massive general-purpose data or auxiliary reward models. In this paper, we argue that, because safety features are inherently sparse within the output distribution, alignment requires localized modifications rather than global trade-offs. To this end, we propose SafeSteer, which performs on-
multiSourceItems.summary: Aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) with human values often degrades their general capabilities, termed the alignment tax. Existing methods mitigate this by balancing dual objectives, which heavily rely on massive general-purpose data or auxiliary reward models. In this paper, we argue that, because safety features are inherently sparse within the output distribution, alignment requires localized modifications rather than global trade-offs. To this end, we propose SafeSteer, which performs on-policy distillation confined to safety tokens. First, we construct a safety teacher via activation steering. Based on this teacher, we develop a safety token selection algorithm. Consequently, SafeSteer restricts the reverse KL penalty to these tokens during training to preserve general capabilities. Experimental results across diverse models show that our SafeSteer achieves a superior trade-off between safety and general capability compared with existing methods, attaining strong safety performance on seven safety benchmarks with only minimal degradation on five general capability benchmarks. Notably, SafeSteer requires only 100 harmful samples without using any general-purpose data, less than 1% of what previous baselines used, considerably reducing alignment cost. More details are on our project page at https://anjingkun.github.io/SafeSteer.
multiSourceItems.author: Hao Li, Jingkun An, Zijun Song, Pengyu Zhu, Rui Li, Hao Wang, Wendi Feng, Yesheng Liu, Lijun Li, Jin-Ge Yao, Lei Sha

multiSourceItems.title: A No-Regret Framework for Adaptive Incentive Design
multiSourceItems.description: Incentive design studies how a central authority can influence strategic agents through payments, subsidies, or taxes, so that individual objectives align with collective welfare. This paper introduces a No-Regret Adaptive Incentive Design (RAID) framework for nonlinear games with continuous action spaces and private agent costs. In this framework, the authority (planner) designs incentives that regulate the Nash equilibrium toward a socially optimal action profile, while simultaneously learning
multiSourceItems.summary: Incentive design studies how a central authority can influence strategic agents through payments, subsidies, or taxes, so that individual objectives align with collective welfare. This paper introduces a No-Regret Adaptive Incentive Design (RAID) framework for nonlinear games with continuous action spaces and private agent costs. In this framework, the authority (planner) designs incentives that regulate the Nash equilibrium toward a socially optimal action profile, while simultaneously learning agents' unknown preferences from repeated strategic responses. We formulate the RAID problem and construct a least-squares estimator whose strong consistency requires only diminishing excitation. Leveraging this weak excitation requirement, we propose a switching incentive policy that alternates between probing (exploration) and estimate-based (exploitation) incentives. The resulting policy achieves an $O(t^{-0.5})$ parameter estimation rate and accumulates $O(t^{0.5}\log t)$ squared social-cost regret, almost surely. We further extend the framework to an endogenous-noise response model, where standard least-squares estimation is biased due to an error-in-variables correlation between the noise and agent responses. We utilize a repeated-sampling estimator and corresponding switching policy that retain the same almost-sure convergence and regret rates. Numerical experiments validate the effectiveness and predicted convergence rates of the method.
multiSourceItems.author: Georgios Vasileiou, Lantian Zhang, Silun Zhang
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, rag

multiSourceItems.title: Auditing Asset-Specific Preferences in Financial Large Language Models: Evidence from Bitcoin Representations and Portfolio Allocation
multiSourceItems.description: Large language models now power robo-advisors and trading agents, yet whether they carry built-in biases toward specific assets is largely untested. We ask three questions: do LLMs systematically prefer certain financial instruments; can an internal representation with causal leverage over those preferences be identified; and does that representation affect downstream financial decisions? We develop a three-level audit protocol and apply it to Bitcoin. First, a behavioral audit of eight frontier
multiSourceItems.summary: Large language models now power robo-advisors and trading agents, yet whether they carry built-in biases toward specific assets is largely untested. We ask three questions: do LLMs systematically prefer certain financial instruments; can an internal representation with causal leverage over those preferences be identified; and does that representation affect downstream financial decisions? We develop a three-level audit protocol and apply it to Bitcoin. First, a behavioral audit of eight frontier LLMs shows that Bitcoin's ranking among money-like instruments is frame-dependent: models place it around rank 5 of 8 as "reliable money" but near the top under crisis and autonomous-agent frames, and an attribute-swap experiment confirms rankings track functional properties, not names. Second, we open a model's internals: a search across thousands of sparse-autoencoder features in Gemma 3 identifies a dominant Bitcoin-selective feature. Amplifying it shifts the model toward the asset and suppressing it shifts the model away, even when "Bitcoin" never appears in the prompt. Third, we test financial consequences: amplification raises Bitcoin's portfolio share by 5.2 percentage points while suppression lowers it by 4.6 pp, with amplification reallocating within crypto and suppression cutting total crypto exposure. We characterize this as bounded behavioral leverage (leverage meaning causal influence over outputs, not financial leverage): an identifiable internal feature can be perturbed to move financial choices, but only within measurable limits. The framework links internal representations to external recommendations, validated with random controls and mechanism boundaries. As LLMs become autonomous financial agents, this is a first step toward a behavioral layer for emerging know-your-agent (KYA) standards: knowing what an agent prefers, and how far that preference can be moved.
multiSourceItems.author: Wenbin Wu
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, agent, rag

multiSourceItems.title: Why Not Hyperparameter-Friendly Optimisation? A Monotonic Adaptive Norm Rescaling Approach For Long-Tailed Recognition
multiSourceItems.description: Long-tailed recognition poses a significant challenge for deep learning. The two-stage decoupling paradigm, which separates representation learning from classifier retraining, offers a promising solution. During the classifier retraining stage, adaptive norm rescaling is a popular technique. It adjusts the per-class weight norms via parameter regularization, which inevitably introduces hyperparameters. However, many studies report that long-tailed recognition is sensitive to these hyperparameter
multiSourceItems.summary: Long-tailed recognition poses a significant challenge for deep learning. The two-stage decoupling paradigm, which separates representation learning from classifier retraining, offers a promising solution. During the classifier retraining stage, adaptive norm rescaling is a popular technique. It adjusts the per-class weight norms via parameter regularization, which inevitably introduces hyperparameters. However, many studies report that long-tailed recognition is sensitive to these hyperparameters, as their setup significantly impacts performance. In this paper, we first provide a class-conditional distribution perspective to support norm rescaling methods. Furthermore, we propose a simple but effective approach called Self-Adaptive Monotonic Normalization (SAMN). SAMN avoids the need for parameter regularization. It directly enforces monotonicity on per-class weight norms using the Pool Adjacent Violators Algorithm, making the method hyperparameter-friendly. SAMN is a universal strategy that integrates seamlessly with other methods for enhanced performance. Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method significantly boosts long-tailed recognition performance, often achieving state-of-the-art results.
multiSourceItems.author: Shuo Zhang, Chenqi Li, Tingting Zhu
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: benchmark

multiSourceItems.description: Suicide memes are memes used to express suicide-related thoughts or comment on suicide-related issues. Suicide memes are increasingly common on social media, yet remain poorly understood and potentially harmful. There is an urgent need to better understand their characteristics and to develop appropriate content moderation strategies that limits users' exposure to potentially harmful content. Currently, the absence of annotated datasets of suicide memes remains a key barrier to developing and ev
multiSourceItems.summary: Suicide memes are memes used to express suicide-related thoughts or comment on suicide-related issues. Suicide memes are increasingly common on social media, yet remain poorly understood and potentially harmful. There is an urgent need to better understand their characteristics and to develop appropriate content moderation strategies that limits users' exposure to potentially harmful content. Currently, the absence of annotated datasets of suicide memes remains a key barrier to developing and evaluating automated moderation approaches. In this paper, we introduce FigSIM, the first dataset designed for fine-grained analysis of suicide memes. The dataset consists of 1049 memes, each annotated for (1) fine-grained suicide severity levels, (2) figurative phenomena (e.g., metaphors), and (3) suicide-related content (e.g., suicide method depiction). We benchmark 16 unimodal and multimodal models across three tasks: figurative language, suicide severity, and suicide-related content detection. Overall, FigSIM demonstrates that suicide memes pose unique challenges for both modeling and content moderation. Analysis revealed biases, such as underprediction of higher suicide severity levels, especially for figurative memes. The dataset (including splits used for analyses) is publicly available. Content Warning: This paper contains suicide-related content that may be triggering.
multiSourceItems.author: Liuliu Chen, Elise R. Carrotte, Brian E. Chapman, Jo Robinson, Mike Conway
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: benchmark

multiSourceItems.title: Moment-Video: Diagnosing Temporal Fidelity of Video MLLMs on Momentary Visual Events
multiSourceItems.description: Video multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have made rapid progress on general and long-form video understanding, yet their ability to preserve brief answer-critical visual evidence remains underexplored. Many practical questions are determined by momentary visual events: localized actions or state transitions that may last only a few frames. Such evidence can be skipped by sparse frame sampling, suppressed by visual-token compression, or diluted by coarse temporal aggregation, causing failu
multiSourceItems.summary: Video multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have made rapid progress on general and long-form video understanding, yet their ability to preserve brief answer-critical visual evidence remains underexplored. Many practical questions are determined by momentary visual events: localized actions or state transitions that may last only a few frames. Such evidence can be skipped by sparse frame sampling, suppressed by visual-token compression, or diluted by coarse temporal aggregation, causing failures that language-side reasoning cannot reliably recover. We introduce Moment-Video, a benchmark for diagnosing the temporal fidelity of video MLLMs through momentary visual event understanding. Each question is grounded in a localized, visually observable, and sampling-sensitive event, requiring models to notice, count, describe, or reason about transient evidence rather than rely on persistent objects, global scene context, or language priors. Moment-Video contains 1,000 human-verified video-QA pairs across 7 domains and 25 fine-grained subcategories, covering four task types: Temporal Occurrence, Temporal Counting, Action Description, and Temporal Reasoning. We evaluate 33 proprietary and open-source MLLMs on Moment-Video. The best-performing model, Seed-2.0-Pro, achieves only 39.6% overall accuracy, while most open-source models remain below 25%, revealing a substantial gap in momentary visual event understanding. Diagnostic analyses show that denser frame sampling improves some models but does not eliminate the bottleneck, and longer videos introduce stronger temporal-localization challenges. These findings suggest that current video MLLMs still lack temporally faithful representations for capturing, preserving, and using brief but decisive visual evidence.
multiSourceItems.author: Xiaolin Liu, Yilun Zhu, Xiangyu Zhao, Xuehui Wang, Yan Li, Xin Li, Haoyu Cao, Xing Sun, Shaofeng Zhang, Xu Yang, Zhihang Zhong, Xue Yang

llmSummary.whatItIs: A CLI proxy that intercepts LLM API calls and caches responses to reduce token usage by 60-90% on common developer commands. Written in Rust as a single binary with no dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: Sustained high star growth (3,627 in 7 days) indicates strong developer interest in cost optimization for LLM usage. The tool addresses a clear pain point as AI-assisted development scales.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers using LLM APIs frequently, engineering managers concerned with API costs, and platform teams building internal AI tooling.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Directly reduces operational costs for teams using LLM APIs in development workflows. Could be integrated into CI/CD pipelines or developer toolchains.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides immediate cost savings with minimal setup. The single-binary Rust approach makes it easy to adopt without additional dependencies.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Caching may lead to stale responses if LLM models update frequently. Effectiveness depends on the repetitiveness of queries. No evidence of production adoption yet.

trendEntities.title: Anthropic 保密向 SEC 提交 S-1 草案
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic 保密向 sec 提交 s 1 草案
title:anthropic confidentially submits draft s 1 to the sec

items.title: Anthropic 保密向 SEC 提交 S-1 草案
items.summary: Anthropic， PBC 今日保密向美国证券交易委员会提交了 S-1 表格草案，计划进行普通股的首次公开发行。这使其在 SEC 完成审核后拥有上市的选择权。IPO 的具体发行股数和价格尚未确定，将取决于市场条件等因素。公司近期刚完成由 Altimeter Capital 等领投的 650 亿美元 H 轮融资，估值达 9650 亿美元，并发布了 Claude Opus 4.8 模型。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic扩展Project Glasswing计划

items.title: Anthropic扩展Project Glasswing计划
items.summary: Anthropic正将其Project Glasswing计划扩展至约150个新组织，此前首批约50个合作伙伴。新伙伴分布于十五个多国家，覆盖电力、水务、医疗、通信和硬件等关键基础设施行业。这些合作伙伴的共同点在于，其代码库若遭成功攻击，后果可能极其严重，影响或超1亿人。项目旨在利用Claude Mythos Preview等前沿模型扫描漏洞并协助修复，以应对AI驱动的网络安全挑战。同时，Anthropic推出了基于Claude Opus 4.8等公开模型的Claude Security产品，用于扫描代码并建议补丁。

sourceItems.title: Anthropic扩展Project Glasswing计划
sourceItems.summary: Anthropic正将其Project Glasswing计划扩展至约150个新组织，此前首批约50个合作伙伴。新伙伴分布于十五个多国家，覆盖电力、水务、医疗、通信和硬件等关键基础设施行业。这些合作伙伴的共同点在于，其代码库若遭成功攻击，后果可能极其严重，影响或超1亿人。项目旨在利用Claude Mythos Preview等前沿模型扫描漏洞并协助修复，以应对AI驱动的网络安全挑战。同时，Anthropic推出了基于Claude Opus 4.8等公开模型的Claude Security产品，用于扫描代码并建议补丁。

items.title: Nathan Lambert离开Ai2，结束2.5年OLMO等项目工作
items.summary: Ai2（Allen Institute for AI）研究员Nathan Lambert宣布离职。他在Ai2工作超过2.5年，期间主导或参与了OLMO和Tulu等开源模型项目，称其为职业生涯的巅峰。他表示将暂时休息，未来仍会继续深耕开源模型与开放科学领域。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic可解释性研究：区分因果效应相似的特征
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic可解释性研究 区分因果效应相似的特征

items.title: Anthropic可解释性研究：区分因果效应相似的特征
items.summary: Anthropic可解释性团队介绍了其Circuits研究的新进展。为区分那些激活模式相似但因果效应不同的模型特征，团队提出一种新方法。该方法通过分析特征的下游连接来预测其实际影响，并使用基于共激活统计的TWERA（虚拟权重）对连接进行加权排序。实验表明，借助下游连接信息能更准确地判断哪个特征会引导特定输出。此方法为识别模型内部真正的因果组件提供了新途径。

sourceItems.title: Anthropic可解释性研究：区分因果效应相似的特征
sourceItems.summary: Anthropic可解释性团队介绍了其Circuits研究的新进展。为区分那些激活模式相似但因果效应不同的模型特征，团队提出一种新方法。该方法通过分析特征的下游连接来预测其实际影响，并使用基于共激活统计的TWERA（虚拟权重）对连接进行加权排序。实验表明，借助下游连接信息能更准确地判断哪个特征会引导特定输出。此方法为识别模型内部真正的因果组件提供了新途径。

trendEntities.title: SK会长崔泰源：SK海力士计划未来五年内晶圆产能翻倍
normalizedKeys: title:sk会长崔泰源 sk海力士计划未来五年内晶圆产能翻倍

items.title: SK会长崔泰源：SK海力士计划未来五年内晶圆产能翻倍
items.summary: SK海力士会长崔泰源宣布，计划在未来五年内将整体晶圆产能提高一倍，以应对AI普及带来的持续存储供应短缺。他预测AI数据中心和AI PC的普及将持续拉动存储需求，供需紧张局面可能延续至2030年。SK海力士将投入大规模资金用于设备、建设等扩张，尽管面临前置时间长（新建晶圆厂至少三年）和资源成本上涨等挑战。目前，SK海力士市值已首次突破1万亿美元。

trendEntities.title: 为了不花那120刀，我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill
normalizedKeys: title:为了不花那120刀 我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill

items.title: 为了不花那120刀，我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill
items.summary: 作者受一条推文启发，使用Codex对自己的MacBook进行了只读存储分析，发现了B站缓存视频等大量可清理空间（激进方案超140G）。为替代收费软件CleanMyMac，作者制作并开源了一个跨平台（支持Mac/Windows）的AI清理skill。该skill会扫描文件并生成可交互的HTML报告，通过三色分级（绿灯可放心清理、黄灯需人工判断、红灯禁止动）直观展示，并提供安全执行按钮。实测清理后释放了近120G空间，相比CleanMyMac仅扫描出的15.8G，其信息更透明、建议更详细。

trendEntities.title: 阶跃星辰Step 3.7 Flash发布，专为高效推理设计
normalizedKeys: title:阶跃星辰step 3 7 flash发布 专为高效推理设计

items.title: 阶跃星辰Step 3.7 Flash发布，专为高效推理设计
items.summary: 阶跃星辰发布其推理优化型模型Step 3.7 Flash。该模型为196B MoE架构，从设计之初就专注于推理效率。其采用多矩阵分解注意力机制，使KV-cache成本仅为DeepSeek模型的约22%；同时通过注意力与FFN解耦技术，实现了硬件优化的高效服务。该模型已通过Fireworks AI提供，采用Apache 2.0许可，并可用于构建智能体应用。

items.title: NVIDIA JetPack 7.2支持内存优化的边缘智能体部署
items.summary: NVIDIA JetPack 7.2发布，支持一键部署开源NVIDIA NemoClaw堆栈，该堆栈为OpenClaw添加了隐私与安全控制。同时引入NVIDIA agent skills for Jetson，为Jetson设备提供智能体技能。该版本优化了内存效率，旨在加速AI代理从数字世界向物理环境的边缘部署。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic 开发者分享 Claude Code「理解验证」工作流
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic 开发者分享 claude code 理解验证 工作流

items.title: Anthropic 开发者分享 Claude Code「理解验证」工作流
items.summary: Anthropic 核心开发者分享了一套用于 Claude Code 的「理解验证」工作流。该工作流将 AI 定位为"高效且睿智的教师"，成功标准不仅是完成任务，更是确保人类对问题、方案及影响有可复述、可辩护的掌握。它通过增量教学、用户复述、清单+测验等方式，围绕问题域、方案域和语境域三条轴线展开，具体包含8个可执行步骤，强调在进入下一阶段前需确认用户已真正理解。此工作流旨在对抗长会话中人类易沦为"审批按钮"的"智能体黑箱"问题，强制沉淀决策上下文，实现可审计的深度理解。

trendEntities.title: Sam Altman强调AI发展应以人为本
normalizedKeys: title:sam altman强调ai发展应以人为本

items.title: Sam Altman强调AI发展应以人为本
items.summary: Sam Altman在采访中表示，AI不应被设计为追求脱离人类需求的目标，人类必须始终处于AI发展的中心。他批判了行业内"AI将摧毁大量工作"等言论，认为人们担忧的并非AI带来的好处，而是自身在未来的角色、经济前景与自主权。他指出，AI行业的失败在于未能清晰解释人类如何在每一步保持对未来的控制权，以及如何在AI时代继续拥有充实、有意义的生活。

items.title: SenseNova新模型解决AI图表生成难题
items.summary: 大多数AI模型在生成图表时存在数值错误（如负值显示为正）、柱状图位置偏移、元素关系混乱等问题。SenseNova-U1-8B-MoT-Infographic（SenseNova-U1）专为解决此类图表生成问题而设计，能够生成准确的图表，并支持实时调整设计和布局。项目在Hugging Face提供了模型，并在GitHub展示了效果案例。

trendEntities.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
normalizedKeys: title:mitigating perceptual judgment bias in multimodal llm as a judge via perceptual perturbation and reward modeling

items.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
items.description: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response
items.summary: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response text instead of their own visual perception, leading to inconsistent and non-verifiable evaluations. To address this issue, we introduce the Perceptually Perturbed Judgment Dataset, which constructs minimally edited counterfactual responses that isolate perceptual errors and enable verifiable supervision. Building on this dataset, we develop a unified training framework that combines a structured GRPO-based reward with a batch-ranking objective, achieving coherent global ordering without explicit pairwise labels. Experiments across diverse MLLM-as-a-Judge benchmarks show that our approach substantially improves perceptual fidelity, ranking coherence, and alignment with human evaluation. Our results establish a scalable and generalizable pathway for training multimodal judges that are perceptually grounded, interpretable, and robust to visual-reasoning conflicts.
items.author: Seojeong Park, Jiho Choi, Junyong Kang, Seonho Lee, Jaeyo Shin, Hyunjung Shim
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, alignment, benchmark, evaluation

sourceItems.summary: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response text instead of their own visual perception, leading to inconsistent and non-verifiable evaluations. To address this issue, we introduce the Perceptually Perturbed Judgment Dataset, which constructs minimally edited counterfactual responses that isolate perceptual errors and enable verifiable supervision. Building on this dataset, we develop a unified training framework that combines a structured GRPO-based reward with a batch-ranking objective, achieving coherent global ordering without explicit pairwise labels. Experiments across diverse MLLM-as-a-Judge benchmarks show that our approach substantially improves perceptual fidelity, ranking coherence, and alignment with human evaluation. Our results establish a scalable and generalizable pathway for training multimodal judges that are perceptually grounded, interpretable, and robust to visual-reasoning conflicts.
sourceItems.author: Seojeong Park, Jiho Choi, Junyong Kang, Seonho Lee, Jaeyo Shin, Hyunjung Shim

items.title: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
items.description: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong performance through instruction tuning, but real-world deployment requires them to continually acquire new vision-language capabilities, making Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) essential.
